title: Export to Excel - fixing formatting, branding and other issues
excerpt:
We have had difficulty working with Excel sheets exported from monday.

Change Fonts

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have had difficulty working with Excel sheets exported from monday.

I finally put together a set of table-driven macros for Excel that can perform the following functions:

  • Remove Branding, Add a Logo
  • Change Fonts
  • Set Number Format, Fix Dates
  • Delete Columns
  • Set Alignments, Column Widths, Row Heights, Wrap Text

I’m looking to see if there is any interest in the community. It’s been an interesting project and I might considering putting in more.

If you are interested and trying what I have so far, let me know a little bit of what you are looking for and I’ll give a few out.

posts.author: CAllen (Carol Allen)
posts.cookedHtml: I would be interested in this. Can you give more info about it?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@CAllen, basically, you set up the commands that you want to run for each particular board. You can also have commands that run on all boards. Once the set is done it can be edited but does not need to be touched again.

When a board is exported and opened in Excel, you click one button to run the commands. It’s working very well for us. At this point, I’m just looking for feedback on how other companies like it and what else they would like it to do.

I’ll try and put together a video to show how it works in the next couple of day.

posts.author: Marton (Marton Varga)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know it’s not the perfect solution but you can record any macro you would like to in Excel.

In order to do that first Developer mode should be enabled:
On the File tab, go to Options > Customize Ribbon. Under Customize the Ribbon and under Main Tabs, select the Developer check box. Click Ok.

After that on the Developer tab, click Record Macro. Optionally, enter a name for the macro in the Macro name box, enter a shortcut key in the Shortcut key box, and a description in the Description box, and then click OK to start recording.

Right click on Monday banner and select Cut. Click Stop Macro.

Next time just hit the short key you’ve selected and the banner will be cut.

Similarly any other must-be-repeated-every-time modification on the table can be recorder and saved for the future.
